title: Sudden severe abdominal pain in a 60-year-old on NSAIDs
presentation: A 60-year-old female on long-term ibuprofen for rheumatoid arthritis presents with sudden onset severe epigastric pain that became diffuse over 2 hours. She describes it as the worst pain of her life. Vitals: BP 100/70, HR 122, RR 22, Temp 37.8C, SpO2 98%. Exam: rigid board-like abdomen, guarding, rebound tenderness throughout, absent bowel sounds.

gold_standard_workup.key_decision_point: Free air under diaphragm on upright CXR = perforation until proven otherwise. Surgical emergency — do not delay for extensive workup. NSAID use + epigastric pain = perforated peptic ulcer most likely.
